coordinate_provenance_records.site_label: Near East and Europe pig domestication transect
coordinate_provenance_records.original_place_text: Near East and Europe pig domestication transect
coordinate_provenance_records.resolved_place_text: Near East to Europe regional study extent
coordinate_provenance_records.geocoder_or_gazetteer: not applied because the current lead is transregional
coordinate_provenance_records.confidence_rationale: The archived source supports a domestication transect across the Near East and Europe rather than one site, so point-level mapping would bluff precision.
coordinate_provenance_records.interpretation_note: Region-level pig turnover evidence is kept as non-mappable context until site rows exist.
coordinate_provenance_records.support_gap_note: The current pig lead is explicitly regional and therefore refused from point publication.

site_evidence_records.site_label: Near East and Europe pig domestication transect
site_evidence_records.exact_source_text: Pig domestication had begun by ~10,500 y before the present (BP) in the Near East, and mitochondrial DNA suggests that pigs arrived in Europe alongside farmers ~8,500 y BP.
site_evidence_records.interpretation_note: This is a broad domestication transect, not one excavation site, so the atlas point remains an inferred regional centroid.

normalization_bundle.normalization_scope: Non-human normalization currently governs accession-backed sample master rows, project summaries, study summaries, and curated locality summaries. Site and chronology extraction still vary by accession and remain explicit in sample inclusion status rather than being silently flattened.
